summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened part five of the series by reading James 5:7-11, focusing on the command to 'be patient' and 'stand firm.' He shared his personal testimony of suffering a massive hemorrhagic stroke just days after releasing Impact Worship's first song, 'He Is the Miracle,' to illustrate that God is still in the miracle-working business. The central message, titled 'Waiting Time Is Not Wasted Time,' was directed at anyone in a season of waiting—for healing, a promise, a breakthrough, or a miracle. Pastor Travis acknowledged that waiting is difficult in a fast-paced, instant-gratification culture, but he reframed it as an active, purposeful season where God is at work. He offered two main reasons for this: first, God is working while you're waiting, using the analogy of a farmer who actively tends the field in expectation of a harvest. Second, the weight of waiting is worth it, as hardships and burdens spiritually strengthen us, much like lifting weights builds physical muscle. He connected this to the ultimate weight Jesus bore on the cross for our salvation. The sermon concluded with a call for salvation and a declaration of faith that God will perform miracles for those who wait actively on Him.
mainPoints: Waiting time is not wasted time; it is an active season of investment and positioning where God is at work.
God uses the weight and pressure of waiting seasons to develop our character, strengthen our faith, and prepare us for His purpose.
The ultimate example of bearing a heavy weight is Jesus on the cross, who carried the sins of the world to give us rest and salvation, proving that the wait is worth it.

versesMentioned.text: Be patient, then, brothers and sisters, until the Lord’s coming. See how the farmer waits for the land to yield its valuable crop, patiently waiting for the autumn and spring rains. You too, be patient and stand firm, because the Lord’s coming is near. Don’t grumble against one another, brothers and sisters, or you will be judged. The Judge is standing at the door! Brothers and sisters, as an example of patience in the face of suffering, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord. As you know, we count as blessed those who have persevered. You have heard of Job’s perseverance and have seen what the Lord finally brought about. The Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
For the revelation awaits an appointed time; it speaks of the end and will not prove false. Though it linger, wait for it; it will certainly come and will not delay.
but those who hope in the LORD will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.
But he said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me.

Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.

Monday.scripture: James 5:7 (NIV) - 'Be patient, then, brothers and sisters, until the Lord’s coming. See how the farmer waits for the land to yield its valuable crop, patiently waiting for the autumn and spring rains.'
Monday.action: Identify one area where you are waiting on God. Write down one active step of faith you can take today (like prayer, worship, or serving) while you wait.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to wait actively today, trusting that You are working even when I don't see results. Give me the patience of a farmer.

Tuesday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 (NIV) - 'Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.'
Tuesday.action: Do one act of kindness or 'good' for someone else without expecting anything in return, as an act of faith in God's timing.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, when I grow weary, renew my strength. Help me to persevere in doing good, trusting Your promise of a harvest at the proper time.

Wednesday.scripture: Isaiah 40:31 (NIV) - 'but those who hope in the LORD will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles...'
Wednesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in prayer or worship, specifically asking God to renew your strength and hope as you wait on Him.
Wednesday.prayer: God, I place my hope in You today. Renew my strength. Lift me above my circumstances and help me to soar in faith.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 (NIV) - 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.'
Thursday.action: Acknowledge a current weakness or burden to a trusted friend or in your journal. Ask God to show His strength in that area.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, thank You that Your power is perfected in my weakness. I boast in my need for You today. Let Your grace be enough for me.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 11:28-30 (NIV) - 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.'
Friday.action: Intentionally lay down a specific worry or burden you are carrying. Verbally give it to Jesus and choose not to pick it back up today.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I come to You weary and burdened. I give You [name the burden]. I take Your yoke upon me and receive Your rest for my soul.

Saturday.scripture: Habakkuk 2:3 (NIV) - 'For the revelation awaits an appointed time; it speaks of the end and will not prove false. Though it linger, wait for it; it will certainly come and will not delay.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on a promise or vision God has given you. Write a declaration of faith that you will wait for it with confident expectation.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I thank You that Your vision for my life is for an appointed time. Though it lingers, I choose to wait for it with faith, believing it will surely come.
